### Fix 3
Line: 103
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-menuitem]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <MenuItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <MenuItem> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <MenuItem variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></MenuItem>
  After:  <MenuItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <MenuItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <MenuItem><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</MenuItem>
  After:  <MenuItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</MenuItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: MenuItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: MenuList

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: MenuItem
Prop: icon

  103                  <MenuItem
  104                    onClick={() =>

```

### Fix 4
Line: 18
Rule [sd-test-searchinput-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
SearchInput is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- SearchInput renders Button which has a behavioral change: aria-expanded attribute added to <Component> in Button
- SearchInput renders Button which has a behavioral change: aria-disabled attribute removed from <Component> in Button
- SearchInput renders Button which has a behavioral change: role on <Component> in Button changed from '{isInlineSpan ? 'button' : null}' to '{isInlineSpan ? 'button' : role}'
- SearchInput renders Button which has a behavioral change: Internal wrapper structure around children in Button changed from Component to span
- SearchInput renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for SearchInput may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Incident context:
  importedName: SearchInput
  localName: SearchInput
  module: @patternfly/react-core

   18    SearchInput,
   19    Title,

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/virtualmachines/details/tabs/configuration/search/VirtualMachineConfigurationTabSearch.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1 — Remove `Icon` import (line 12)
`Icon` (from `EmptyStateIcon`/`IconProps`) is removed in PF v6. Looking at lines 116-118, `Icon` is used as a wrapper around `SearchIcon`. In PF v6, the `Icon` component no longer exists. The usage `<Icon color="grey" size="xl"><SearchIcon /></Icon>` needs to be replaced. Since `Icon` was from EmptyState's icon system, and the usage here is just to style an icon with color and size, I should remove the `Icon` wrapper and apply styling directly to `SearchIcon` (which accepts `color` and `size` as props from react-icons).

### Fix 2 — `Menu` `onSelect` type change (line 99)
The `onSelect` prop type changed from `(event?: MouseEvent, itemId?: string | number) => void` to `(event?: MouseEvent, itemId?: MenuItemProps['itemId']) => void`. Looking at the current `onSelect` handler (line 86), the `itemId` parameter is typed as `string`. The `MenuItemProps['itemId']` type is `string | number | undefined`. This should be compatible, but I should update the handler's type signature to match. The key change: `itemId: string` → `itemId: string | number`.

### Fix 3 — `MenuItem` icon children to prop (line 103)
Looking at lines 103-112, the `MenuItem` usage has `{element?.title}` as children — this is text, not an icon. There's no icon element inside this `MenuItem`. This fix doesn't apply to this specific `MenuItem` usage since it only contains text children, not an icon from `@patternfly/react-icons`.

### Fix 4 — SearchInput behavioral changes (line 18)
This is an informational/test-impact rule about SearchInput's rendered sub-components having behavioral changes. No code change needed in the source file — this only affects tests. There are no test files listed.

### Fix 5 — `Popper` props signature changed (line 17)
`PopperProps` now extends `PopperOptions`. This is a base class addition. The current usage of `Popper` (lines 97-138) uses `popper`, `trigger`, and `isVisible` props. These are standard Popper props that should still work. No code change needed for this usage.

Now let me apply the changes:

arguments.before:
import {
  Bullseye,
  Icon,
  Menu,
  MenuContent,
  MenuItem,
  MenuList,
  Popper,
  SearchInput,
  Title,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';

arguments.after: const onSelect = (e: MouseEvent<Element, globalThis.MouseEvent>, itemId: string | number) => {
arguments.before: const onSelect = (e: MouseEvent<Element, globalThis.MouseEvent>, itemId: string) => {

content.text: Now update the `setValue(itemId)` call — since `setValue` expects a `string` and `itemId` is now `string | number`, I need to convert:
Now replace the `<Icon>` wrapper with direct props on `SearchIcon`:
Now let me verify the final state of the file:
The file looks correct. Let me verify there are no companion test files to check:

No companion test files found. The file is complete and correct.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Icon` component removed (line 12)
**Applied.** Removed `Icon` from the `@patternfly/react-core` import. Replaced `<Icon color="grey" size="xl"><SearchIcon /></Icon>` with `<SearchIcon color="grey" size="xl" />`, passing the `color` and `size` props directly to the `SearchIcon` component (react-icons components accept these props natively).

### Fix 2 — `Menu` `onSelect` type change (line 99)
**Applied.** Updated the `onSelect` handler's `itemId` parameter type from `string` to `string | number` to match the new PF v6 signature `(event?: MouseEvent, itemId?: MenuItemProps['itemId']) => void`. Also wrapped the `setValue(itemId)` call with `String(itemId)` to safely convert to the `string` type expected by `useState<string>`.

### Fix 3 — `MenuItem` icon children to prop (line 103)
**No change needed.** The `MenuItem` at line 103 contains only text children (`{element?.title}`), not an icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons`. The rule's condition (icon content as children) does not match this usage.

### Fix 4 — `SearchInput` transitive behavioral changes (line 18)
**No change needed.** This is an informational/test-impact rule about behavioral changes in SearchInput's rendered sub-components (Button, Popper). No source code changes are required — this only affects tests, and no companion test files exist for this component.

### Fix 5 — `Popper` `PopperProps` signature changed (line 17)
**No change needed.** The `PopperProps` interface now extends `PopperOptions` (additive base class change). The current usage of `Popper` with `popper`, `trigger`, and `isVisible` props is compatible with the new signature. No props need to be added or changed.

Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-menuitem]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <MenuItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <MenuItem> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <MenuItem variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></MenuItem>
  After:  <MenuItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <MenuItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <MenuItem><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</MenuItem>
  After:  <MenuItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</MenuItem>

Rule [sd-test-searchinput-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
SearchInput is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- SearchInput renders Button which has a behavioral change: aria-expanded attribute added to <Component> in Button
- SearchInput renders Button which has a behavioral change: aria-disabled attribute removed from <Component> in Button
- SearchInput renders Button which has a behavioral change: role on <Component> in Button changed from '{isInlineSpan ? 'button' : null}' to '{isInlineSpan ? 'button' : role}'
- SearchInput renders Button which has a behavioral change: Internal wrapper structure around children in Button changed from Component to span
- SearchInput renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for SearchInput may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

**Applied.** Updated the `onSelect` handler's `itemId` parameter type from `string` to `string | number` to match the new PF v6 signature `(event?: MouseEvent, itemId?: MenuItemProps['itemId']) => void`. Also wrapped the `setValue(itemId)` call with `String(itemId)` to safely convert to the `string` type expected by `useState<string>`.
